intro: Asia-Pacific firms are investing heavily in AI despite limited financial returns, driven primarily by competitive anxiety rather than proven value. President Trump signed an executive order requesting voluntary early access to frontier AI models from developers, alongside a scaled-back cybersecurity review process. Alphabet announced plans to raise $80 billion for AI infrastructure, including an $80 million investment from Berkshire Hathaway and a $40 billion at-the-market offering.

whitelist.title: Fear of missing out: why Asia-Pacific firms chase AI despite scant returns
whitelist.desc: Survey shows many enterprises across the region invest heavily in AI, worried that their rivals will outpace them.
